title: Determining the Display Device
text: Determining the Display Device Uniface use the several mechanisms to determine which device translation table to use for display. Assignment files—if the device translation table you want to use is not in the default library (USYS), the $VARIATION and $DISPLAY settings must specify the library and device translation table to use for screen output. If specific applications use different values, it is better to define them in a Uniface assignment file than rely on the other mechanisms. Environment variables—the UDISP variable, if defined, overrides the system default for the display device. If the devices used are applicable for everyone at your site, it is advisable to use operating system symbols to define the assignments. These assignments should be placed in a system-wide logon script, if there is one. Refer to the documentation for your operating system to see how to do this. Operating system defaults—for example, on Unix the TERM environment variable indicates terminal being used. If no other assignments are present, Uniface uses the default obtained from the operating system to determine the device translation table used. Setting UDISP The UDISP symbol overrides the system display default. It has different names under each operating system and is set in different ways. For example: Under Windows, set the UDISP environment variable. Under the Unix shell (sh) , use the following command to set the environment variable: UDISP=SUN;export UDISP Copy Uppercase variables have priority over lowercase ones. Refer to the operating system documentation for details. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
